repo.product: Shipping Savior — logistics platform: BOL OCR, shipment tracking, calculators, workbook intake, Stripe billing; web + native mobile
repo.head: d5b4881

invariants: Mobile auth reuses web session cookie (cookieName contract: __Secure-authjs.session-token prod / authjs.session-token dev) so all ~48 auth()-gated routes work unchanged — never retrofit per-route Bearer tokens
BOL uploads capped at 25MB, loaded in memory — do not raise without streaming parse
Prod migrations 0004 (workbook enum) + 0005 (push_tokens) are UNAPPLIED — runtime will crash on push-token insert until Julian migrates
AUTH_URL in Vercel prod contains a trailing newline (OAuth callback breakage) — Julian ops fix
Stripe env vars/products/webhook not configured — revenue blocked, Julian ops

session_boot_sequence.cmd: npm run typecheck && npm run test:run
session_boot_sequence.expect: green baseline; if red before changes: stop, escalate

tasks.title: Rate-limit mobile login + validate Expo push token format

steps.action: reject push tokens not matching /^Expo(nent)?PushToken\[[\w-]+\]$/ with 400

verification: Vitest: 6th rapid login -> 429; malformed token -> 400; valid -> 200

tasks.title: POST /api/mobile/auth/refresh endpoint

steps.action: validate current unexpired token -> issue fresh JWT via same AUTH_SECRET path as login; audit-log channel:'mobile'

verification: valid token -> new token with later expiry; expired/garbage -> 401; cookie-name contract unchanged

tasks.title: Build real mobile screens: login -> shipments dashboard -> BOL camera scan

steps.action: login screen -> POST /api/mobile/auth/login -> store token in expo-secure-store -> attach as session cookie header on all requests

verification: cd mobile && npx expo start --web renders screens
auth flow works against local npm run dev (eas.json dev profile -> localhost:3000)
guardrails: token travels as cookie header, NOT Bearer

tasks.title: OCR provider-fallback observability

steps.action: log each provider failure (provider, error class, latency) to model_comparison_logs + Sentry context; expose winning provider in result

verification: unit test: first provider fails -> log record present + fallback succeeds

tasks.title: One-command Julian ops runbook (dry-run scripts, DO NOT execute on prod)
deliverables: scripts/prod-migrate-check.sh (pending migrations + post-migrate verification SQL for push_tokens table)
AUTH_URL fix instructions using printf (no trailing newline)

Stripe checklist: STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET, NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY, STRIPE_PRICE_PREMIUM_MONTHLY, STRIPE_PRICE_ENTERPRISE_MONTHLY; products (Premium $499/mo); webhook https://shipping-savior.vercel.app/api/billing/webhook
verification: scripts dry-run by default; mutation gated behind CONFIRM=PROD

tasks.title: Export git bundle + session report
verification: bundle path included in session report

tool_protocol.e2e: npm run e2e (after npm run e2e:install once)

hard_stops: running db:migrate/db:push against production Neon (Julian-only, after backup)
adding remotes/tokens/credential helpers to work around missing push creds — bundle instead
per-route Bearer-token retrofit (breaks the cookie reuse design)
logging/committing secrets (AUTH_SECRET, provider keys, Neon URLs)
raising 25MB BOL cap without streaming parse
stubbing fake Stripe keys to force checkout green
third retry of an approach that failed twice

escalation.channel: iMessage session report to +16195090699 per fleet SOP
